metadata.title: Why Two AIs Checking Each Other Is One Blind Spot Twice
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Computational Skepticism for AI candidate 08. Source chapter: books/computational-skepticism-for-ai/chapters/13-accountability-who-is-responsible-when-the-system-fails.md (common cause failure section only).
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

style_bible.visual_style: editorial paper collage on real newsprint scan; desaturated plates; flat annotation plane; serif labels with hairline underlines; sieve metaphor as the through-line visual object
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, charcoal serif type, teal = the valid independent check / what should stop flaws, crimson = the flaw / correlated blind spot / the bad grain, gold highlighter as the single editor's-pen voice

metadata.color_semantics: teal #1F6F5C = the genuine independent check / what is kept and working; crimson #BF3339 = the correlated flaw / blind spot / bad grain (the villain); never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Card exclusions honored: NO Godel incompleteness formalism (the sieve carries the logic), NO seven-tier taxonomy vocabulary, NO chain-of-thought-monitoring literature, NO FDA/aviation case histories beyond one spoken aside in B08. Mechanism only: a checker built on the same data and architecture as the generator has correlated blind spots — the exact error most likely to fool one is most likely to fool both.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Adding a second AI to check the first feels like a safety net. It is the same net with the same hole.

card.copy:
Why Two AIs Checking Each Other
Is One Blind Spot Twice

beats.narration_text: A model generates an output. A second model reviews it. Both trained on the same data, built on the same foundation.

props.headline: A model generates an output. A second model reviews it. Both…

beats.scene_description: two identical-looking neural-network diagrams side by side on aged newsprint — one labeled 'generator', one labeled 'checker' — connected to the same cloud of training data above them; desaturated editorial illustration
beats.image_prompt: two identical layered-network diagrams side by side, each with the same arrangement of nodes and weights, connected by arrows to a shared 'Training Data' cloud above; editorial infographic pinned to aged newsprint, desaturated flat print reproduction, charcoal ink lines on cream paper
beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] two identical models from one training cloud

beats.narration_text: Think of the generator as a sieve. Outputs pass through the mesh. A flawed one should catch — but some slip through the holes.

production_viz.label: generator as sieve — one hole, one bad grain
production_viz.mechanic: a teal sieve bar (two halves with a gap at center) fades in with a 'generator' label above; a crimson grain dot descends from above, passes through the gap and exits below with a 'flaw' label
production_viz.note: establish the sieve metaphor — the hole is always at center

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] one sieve, one bad grain falls through the hole

beats.narration_text: Now place a checker beneath it. A second sieve. The flaw must clear two layers. That is the safety claim.

production_viz.label: second sieve added — the safety claim
production_viz.mechanic: first sieve (generator) at top; second sieve (checker) slides up from below and locks in beneath it; the crimson grain pauses between the two sieves
production_viz.note: the checker slides in — the compare move begins

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] second sieve slides in below — grain paused

beats.narration_text: The problem is what both sieves have in common. They came from the same foundry. Same data, same architecture.

document.quote: What fools the generator is most likely to fool the checker.

beats.narration_text: The holes line up. The flaw slides through the first sieve — and straight through the second one too. Every time.

production_viz.label: aligned holes — the grain falls through both
production_viz.mechanic: two stacked sieves with holes at the same x-position; a dashed crimson alignment line marks the shared hole; the crimson grain drops through sieve 1 then sieve 2 without catching — THE COMPARE MOVE
production_viz.note: manim move: compare — the key reveal of the reel

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] holes aligned — grain falls through both sieves

beats.narration_text: Both models trained on identical data. Their errors are correlated. Whatever the generator gets subtly wrong, the checker is most likely to miss.

production_viz.label: shared training data feeds both — correlated errors
production_viz.mechanic: a slate 'Training Data' box at top; arrows fork down to a teal 'Generator' box (left) and a teal 'Checker' box (right); a crimson 'blind spot' chip appears in the training data, then a matching crimson label appears beneath both model boxes
production_viz.note: the mechanism — same data, same blind spot baked into both

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] training data forks into both models — correlated blind spots

beats.narration_text: High-stakes systems require something genuinely outside. Aircraft certification, clinical trials — a different kind of check, from a different foundation entirely.
beats.scene_description: two stacked sieves (both teal, holes aligned) on the left with the crimson grain falling through; on the right, a different solid bar (teal, no hole) labeled 'independent'; a dividing line separates the two panels; the grain stops at the solid bar

production_viz.label: same foundation vs genuinely outside
production_viz.mechanic: left panel: two sieves with aligned holes, grain passes through; right panel: solid teal bar (no hole) representing an independent check, grain stops; serif labels 'same foundation' (crimson) and 'independent' (teal)
production_viz.note: one spoken aside: aircraft/clinical trials — no detailed case history

beats.new_visual_element: [COMPOSITE] same foundation (flaw through) vs independent check (flaw caught)

beats.narration_text: Same foundation: the flaw slides through. Different foundation: it stops. Redundancy is not the same thing as independence.

production_viz.label: compare: correlated vs independent
production_viz.mechanic: two side-by-side vertical tracks; left track: two sieves aligned, grain drops through; right track: one sieve + one solid bar, grain stops; labels 'redundant' (crimson) and 'independent' (teal)
production_viz.note: the contrast lands here — motion: compare

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] left: grain through / right: grain stopped

beats.narration_text: Stack as many correlated checkers as you want. Each one adds the same hole. The count goes up. The coverage does not.

production_viz.label: stack of aligned sieves — same hole multiplied
production_viz.mechanic: three sieves stack one by one (the accumulate move), each with holes at the same position; a crimson grain is released and drops straight through all three; a counter below ticks up as each sieve adds but the hole stays open
production_viz.note: the payoff — more sieves, same hole

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] three sieves stacked, grain falls through all three

beats.narration_text: A content platform builds a generator model to flag harmful posts and a checker model to review the flags. Both trained on the same moderation corpus. A new harassment pattern emerges in the test set. The generator sees nothing unusual — passes it. The checker, sharing the same training distribution, also sees nothing unusual — confirms the pass. The second model does not add coverage. It adds a second copy of the same blind spot.
A second sieve from the same foundry is not a check. It is a copy.

props.headline: A second sieve from the same foundry is not a check.…

card.sub: The second model has the same blind spot.

beats.name: OutroSeries
beats.narration_text: Part of the Computational Skepticism for AI series.
